channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: MindsEye Dev Lays Off More Staff Days After ‘New DLC’ Drops
item.content:encoded:
Build a Rocket Boy, the troubled developer behind MindsEye, one of the most monumental gaming flops of all time, has reportedly made more layoffs amid an ongoing downturn. In recent days, the team pushed out the Blacklisted ‘DLC’, a new in-game mission that was supposed to be a cryptic uncovering of those trying to sabotage BARB and MindsEye.

Per reports, this is the third wave of redundancies for BARB, which recently suggested a resurgence was in the books for MindsEye, one of the worst-rated games ever made. It has been claimed that up to 170 employees have been impacted, which leaves BARB teetering dangerously on the edge of boasting a skeleton crew.

Can Build a Rocket Boy Ever Recover?

Build a Rocket Boy’s fortunes have gone from bad to worse over the last couple of years. MindsEye wasn’t the success it was hoping for, and the game’s launch was marred by incredibly negative reviews.

I played MindsEye just a few weeks back and generally enjoyed the game, but the reputation of the studio has been hampered by so much bad press that a positive review was a drop in the ocean of disruption.

In a new report from Kotaku, it was claimed that as many as 170 employees were impacted by another round of layoffs at BARB. On LinkedIn, dozens of former employees lamented a restructuring that knocked out multiple departments. Earlier this year, BARB closed an entire studio, after making redundant vast swathes of workers.

Mark Gerhard, the co-CEO of the company alongside Leslie Benzies of Rockstar fame, has been busy condemning the world and everyone in it. He remains adamant that BARB was targeted in a million-dollar smear campaign perpetrated by an influencer management agency, and that’s what the core of Blacklisted, the new ‘DLC’, was supposed to be about.

It’s one of the strangest and most convoluted stories in recent years, and this latest chapter could represent the final nail in the coffin for Build a Rocket Boy.

Do you think BARB can ever recover? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Fortnite Star Wars Droid Tycoon BB-8 Event: Exact Start Time & Date
item.content:encoded:
Star Wars Droid Tycoon has quickly become one of the most popular modes across all of Fortnite, and the upcoming BB-8 event is set to add even more content to the mode.

Epic Games‘ close relationship with Disney has opened the door to a host of Star Wars-themed creative maps, with Droid Tycoon having the support of Epic Labs directly, helping to make it an instant hit.

BB-8’s arrival in Droid Tycoon is the first major event in the mode, and Insider Gaming has all the details on when it takes place.

When is The Star Wars Droid Tycoon BB-8 Event?

The Star Wars Droid Tycoon BB-8 event is set to debut on Sunday, March 10, but the exact duration of the event is unknown.

A precise countdown for exactly how many days, hours, and minutes until BB-8’s arrival in Droid Tycoon can be found outside the Cantina, on the right-hand side, so you’ll always know how much longer you need to wait. However, it doesn’t detail the exact time and date, instead leaving it for you to work out.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Star Wars Droid Tycoon Rebirth Requirements

The countdown shows the Star Wars Droid Tycoon BB-8 event in Fortnite is set to begin at 7 AM PT on Sunday, March 10. The exact times for timezones across the world is shown in the table below.

What’s in The Star Wars Droid Tycoon BB-8 Event?

The full details of the BB-8 event in Star Wars Droid Tycoon are not yet known, but we do know that it will see the debut of BB-8 into the mode.

While there is a host of recognisable Droids in Droid Tycoon, there are some eyecatching absentees, with BB-8, a fan-favorite from sequel triology, not yet in the game.

Its arrival is confirmed, however, and there’s also a tease of what the Gold, Diamond, and Rainbow versions of BB-8 will look like in the game.

More details will likely be revealed in the build-up to the event, and there’s the chance that future events will bring even more Droids into the game.

Are you enjoying Star Wars Droid Tycoon in Fortnite? Share your thoughts on the mode in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Droid Tycoon, check out details of the recent update making grinding for Upgrade Chips easier, and find out how to get a Ground Mech.

item.title: 007 First Light Cast Revealed—All Voice Actors, Explained
item.content:encoded:
James Bond films are renowned for featuring some of the most prominent celebrities across the cast, with high-profile actors typically taking centre stage. Now that the 007 brand is returning to video-game form, it’s bound to get people wondering who’s taking on the crucial roles of Bond and his allies.

Once you pick up the game, you’re bound to recognise some characters, if not by their voice, then certainly by their likeness, as 007 First Light has managed to accrue a Bond-tier cast itself. Check them all out below, as well as where you might know them from.

All Major Voice Actors In 007 First Light

007 First Light has a solid voice cast full of recognisable actors, though the main man himself, James Bond, might be new to you.

The cast has a solid backlog of previous works, with some having worked on the likes of The Walking Dead, Diablo 4, and Andor. As you might notice, a lot of these actors are making their video game debuts, but they’re just as recognisable on the silver screen.

  • More Insider Gaming: Does 007 First Light Have Multiplayer Or Co-Op?

Of course, everyone will want to know who is playing James Bond, and the answer could surprise you. The central role has been filled by Irish actor Patrick Gibson, who makes his video game debut as the voice and likeness of 007.

As for the rest of the cast and characters, you can find them all below:

007 First Light Voice Actors’ Past Roles

If there are any names on the list above that you recognize but can’t quite place your finger on where you know them from, here’s a breakdown of some of the main actors and their previous roles.

Patrick Gibson

With no Bond on the big screen, Patrick Gibson is the one and only 007 for the time being. It’s big boots to fill, but the Irishman has had his fair share of roles in the past.

His most notable role is probably appearing as a young Dexter Morgan in Dexter: Original Sin, though he also had leading roles in Shadow and Bone, as well as The OA.

Priyanga Burford

Funnily enough, this isn’t Priyanga’s first appearance in a Bond project, as she had a small role in No Time To Die, Daniel Craig’s final outing. She was only an MI6 scientist then, but now she’s cast as M, a considerably bigger part.

Historically, she’s made most of her credentials in television, appearing in the likes of Housebound, Avenue 5, Before We Die, and Steeltown Murders.

Lennie James

As for recognisable stars, Lennie James could top the list. He’s got a huge catalogue of work behind him, though fans of The Walking Dead would surely have fond memories of Morgan Jones—he even got his own spinoff, Fear The Walking Dead.

  • More Insider Gaming: All 007 First Light Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices, Explained

He’s appeared in plenty of other popular works, too, with the likes of Blade Runner 2049, Invincible and Archer all popular outings. As for video games, this won’t be his debut, as he voiced Lord Shaxx in Destiny 2.

Kiera Lester

You would be forgiven for not recognising Kiera Lester, as playing Moneypenny in 007 First Light is one of her most prominent roles. She’s appeared in Wreck, Ruby Speaking, and Sister Boniface Mysteries.

Alastair Mackenzie

Alastair Mackenzie tops the cast alongside Lennie James, as the veteran actor has appeared in a variety of acclaimed shows. Andor and The Crown top the charts, but he was also in an episode of Black Mirror.

Noemie Nakai

Noemie Nakai is bound to get a lot of new fans after 007 First Light, as she plays the coveted femme fatale Bond girl. Her most notable previous role was in Tokyo Vice, though she’s made appearances across Army of Thieves and The Emperor’s Cook.

Gemma Chan

Dr Selina Tan is played by Gemma Chan, who has a strong catalogue of prior works, including Captain Marvel, Eternals, and Crazy Rich Asians. She also voiced Erys in Diablo 4, so this won’t be her first video game rodeo.

Lenny Kravitz

Lenny Kravitz is a Grammy-winning musician, but don’t let that fool you, as he’s had plenty of acting roles in the past. His most notable was probably Cinna in the Hunger Games, though he’s of course appeared in tons of his own music videos.

Which of the 007 First Light cast stand out to you the most? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more 007 First Light content, check out the following:

  • 007 First Light Game Pass & Early Access Status, Explained
  • 007 First Light Release Date & All Platforms, Explained
  • How Long Is 007 First Light? Game Length & Total Hours, Explained

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Does 007 First Light Have Multiplayer Or Co-Op?
item.content:encoded:
James Bond is rarely a one-man operation. Whether he’s teaming up with MI6 allies, working alongside fellow agents, or relying on the latest gadgets from Q Division, 007’s missions almost always involve a memorable supporting cast. That’s why many fans are curious whether 007 First Light will let players bring friends along for the ride.

After all, infiltrating enemy compounds and surviving explosive action sequences sounds even more exciting with a partner at your side. However, when you’re trying to stay undercover, an extra agent could easily complicate things. So, does 007 First Light feature multiplayer or co-op gameplay, or is Bond flying solo this time around? Here’s everything you need to know.

Does 007 First Light Have Multiplayer?

Unfortunately, 007 First Light will not feature any form of multiplayer, as the developers said that it “wasn’t in the conversation at all” when developing the game.

Instead, IO Interactive are very determined to create a narrative-based adventure, and to keep it single player would best feed into the studio’s strengths. It might be slightly disappointing for some of you out there, especially fans of the classic N64 Goldeneye multiplayer, but it’s promising to see a studio have such a clear vision for a game.

  • More Insider Gaming: How Long Is 007 First Light? Game Length & Total Hours, Explained

Plus, there will still be some multiplayer elements in 007 First Light, namely regarding the TacSim challenges. These offer replayable scenarios based on the game’s missions, and you can compete in online leaderboards to set high scores. So, whilst you can’t play directly alongside online friends, you can duke it out to see who’s the most effective spy—it’s better than nothing!

Does 007 First Light Have Co-Op?

As you might have guessed, 007 First Light features no co-op gameplay, as there are no other playable characters besides Bond himself.

Whilst couch co-op games are beginning to make a slight comeback, they’re still fairly rare to find. It seems like if you’re looking for an explosive action game to play alongside friends, family or a partner, you’ll have to look elsewhere, as 007 First Light is exclusively single player.

  • More Insider Gaming: All 007 First Light Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices, Explained

Instead, your company would have to settle for watching you play through the game instead. Considering it’s full of action set pieces that are akin to the movies, it might not be such a terrible idea, but only one of you will be in control of the master spy.

Were you hoping to see multiplayer in 007 First Light? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more 007 First Light content, check out the following:

  • 007 First Light Release Date & All Platforms, Explained
  • 007 First Light Game Pass & Early Access Status, Explained

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Valorant Community Pays Tribute to sym Following Fatal Car Accident
item.content:encoded:
The North American Valorant community has paid tribute to Tyler ‘sym’ Porter following a fatal car accident, which took the 21-year-old’s life and the life of the player’s friend, Austen, who was also in the vehicle.

The news was shared in a post by a friend of the player following the fatal accident on May 4th, three days after sym’s 21st Birthday.

Tributes paid to Valorant star after passing

The post stated, “With a broken heart I write this message to everyone that sadly my best friend @symstar and our good friend Austen sadly passed away late last night in a car accident.
“Tyler was celebrating his 21st birthday trip. I know he brought laughs and smiles to a lot of people and If any you have a nice memory or interaction with him please feel free to share it.
“Attached are a few photos of them I hope you might say a prayer for both of them may they both rest in peace.”

The news prompted a post from the player’s former organization, TSM, which stated, “From elevating our Academy program to helping us reach Ascension on the main roster, you left your mark on TSM VALORANT.

“You had so much ahead of you, and we’ll miss you dearly, @symstar. May you and Austen rest in peace.”

sym was best known for his time in TSM, graduating from TSM Academy before taking part in Valorant Challengers League in 2023 as part of Mois Moguls, returning to TSM to compete in VCL for his parent team in 2024 and 2025.

With sym, TSM made Ascension 2024, but failed to qualify for VCT Americas after a 1-4 Group Stage campaign.

The news of his passing prompted hundreds of tributes from the Valorant community, such as Winthrop player Peter ‘Governor’ No, who shared, “Tyler was one of the first people I met in this game that I could call my friend and was always a fun person to be around.

I remember all the LANS we used to go to and the countless hours we’d play val and other variety games. Those were some of the happiest years of my life.”

G2’s Josh ‘JoshRT’ Lee, Fnatic’s Austin ‘crashies’ Roberts, and Shopify Rebellion Gold’s Melanie ‘meL’ Capone were amongst those who shared their condolences.

Shopify Rebellion’s smoke shared the following tribute, saying, “Tyler was one of the most genuine people I’ve met in this scene, and I truly can’t put into words how much he helped me both personally and professionally.”

To discuss anything gaming or esports-related, join our Discord.
For more news, see our VCT EMEA Stage 1 playoff preview.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Star Wars Droid Tycoon Update in Fortnite Makes Grinding Upgrade Chips Easier
item.content:encoded:
Star Wars Droid Tycoon has taken the Fortnite world by storm, and the creators are not resting on their laurels and continue to provide updates to the game, including a tweak to make grinding Upgrade Chips easier.

Upgrade Chips in Droid Tycoon are a crucial resource that are used to upgrade your Droids, without having to wait for higher rarities to spawn at the Jawa Sandcrawler, but the grind to secure the hundreds, and in some cases thousands, required to upgrade some Droids has been unforgiving.

However, a fresh patch bringing Droid Tycoon to Version 13 has changed the formula, providing another quick way to grind for Upgrade Chips.

Upgrade Chips Grind in Star Wars Droid Tycoon Made Easier

As shown in the patch details for Version 13, Star Wars Droid Tycoon now provides Upgrade Chips when selling Gold, Diamond, or Rainbow Droids.

The higher the base rarity of the Droid, the more the amount of Chips you receive for selling them, although the grind can still take a while, as the average is around four to eight Upgrade Chips per Droid.

It is still a considerable improvement to the previous Chip-farming method, however, and shows that the developer is still listening to feedback and is prepared to make considerable updates swiftly.

Upgrading some Droids still takes thousands of Upgrade Chips though, and it remains to be seen whether future updates are planned for late-game content.

Several patches have now been deployed for Droid Tycoon since the game went live, and there’s more to come with the mysterious BB8 event set to take place this weekend.

Are you enjoying Star Wars Droid Tycoon in Fortnite? Share your thoughts on the mode in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Droid Tycoon, find out how to grind Upgrade Chips, and take a look at all the Rebirth requirements.

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For May 6, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, May 6, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Loud
  • Hint 2: Sign of illness
  • Hint 3: Impacts your throat

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Runny
  • Ache
  • Sore
  • Sneeze
  • Sniffle
  • Throat
  • Wheeze
  • Bronchitis
  • Cold

Today’s Contexto Answer For May 6, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Cough

A Cough is a forceful push of air that your body uses to clear irritants out of your airways.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on May 7.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
